outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump and Netanyahu diverge on Iran war's future — Netanyahu urged military action, Trump resisted
outlets.summary: CNN reports on the tense May 20 call between Trump and Netanyahu in which Netanyahu pushed for immediate military resumption and Trump overruled him, signaling growing divergence between US and Israel on war strategy.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran says US proposal has 'narrowed the gaps' as clock ticks on ceasefire
outlets.summary: NPR covers Iran's ISNA statement that the latest US ceasefire text has 'narrowed the gaps to some extent,' the most positive diplomatic signal from Tehran since the April 8 ceasefire.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: China gains major edge on U.S. amid Iran war, U.S. intelligence finds
outlets.summary: WaPo intelligence exclusive: CIA/DIA assessments show China has exploited the Iran war to close technology gaps with the US, expand influence in Gulf states traditionally aligned with Washington, and gain preferential Hormuz transit access.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump at Coast Guard: 'We may have to hit Iran even harder' — war resumption prep intensifies
outlets.summary: Fox covers Trump's Coast Guard Academy speech where he declared the US 'may have to hit Iran even harder' while pursuing diplomacy, as Times of Israel reports US and Israel are in 'most intense preparations yet' for war renewal.

outlets.name: Breitbart
outlets.headline: Vance: 'Locked and loaded' to restart Iran campaign if nuclear talks fail — there is an 'Option B'
outlets.summary: VP Vance reiterates US military readiness to resume campaign against Iran if diplomacy fails, calling Iran's nuclear weapon acquisition an 'absolute red line' while expressing cautious optimism about talks.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: US imposes 13th sanctions round on Iran — construction sector, nuclear materials targeted
outlets.summary: The Hill covers the Treasury and State Department's 13th sanctions package since NSPM-2, targeting IRGC-linked construction companies and restricting nuclear/military-linked materials, imposed simultaneously with active diplomatic talks.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran Says the US's Latest Proposal Has 'Narrowed the Gaps'
outlets.summary: Bloomberg confirms Iran's ISNA report that Tehran is reviewing the latest US ceasefire proposal and that it has 'narrowed the gaps to some extent,' with Iran warning further progress requires Washington to end its 'temptation for war.'

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Israel pushes for war amid US ceasefire, but its options may be limited
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera analysis finds Netanyahu 'itching for war' but constrained by US diplomatic strategy, with analysts noting 'this stops when the US says it stops.' Poll shows majority of Israelis believe ceasefire is against their security interests.

outlets.name: RFERL
outlets.headline: Iran Talks 'In Final Stages' But Threatens Renewed Attacks If No Deal
outlets.summary: Radio Free Europe covers the diplomatic endgame: Iran's foreign minister saying talks are nearing final stages while IRGC simultaneously threatens that renewed US/Israel attacks would trigger a war 'beyond the region.'

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: As Iran talks stall, Israel and US prepping to renew war as soon as next week — report
outlets.summary: Times of Israel/NYT report citing two Middle Eastern officials: US and Israel conducting 'most intense preparations yet' for war renewal, with options including capturing Kharg Island oil hub and a commando operation to extract Iran's enriched uranium stockpile.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Uproar after Channel 14 anchor discloses Iran uranium seizure plan without censor approval
outlets.summary: Haaretz reports the military censorship scandal: Channel 14's Shimon Riklin disclosed details of a commando plan to seize Iran's enriched uranium near Isfahan without IDF censor approval. Former IDF Chief Eisenkot condemned the leak as 'reckless trafficking in national security.'

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Ben-Gvir posts video of bound flotilla detainees — Turkey repatriates 78 nationals, Poland demands EU ban
outlets.summary: Israeli NSM Ben-Gvir's social media post showing Gaza flotilla detainees kneeling with hands bound draws international backlash: Turkey dispatched charter flights to repatriate 78 nationals, Poland's FM demanded Ben-Gvir be banned from EU entry, and EU is reviewing Israel's Association Agreement compliance.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Forcing Iran to surrender 'an illusion' — Pezeshkian says Iran reviewing US proposal
outlets.summary: Press TV covers Pezeshkian's May 21 statement insisting Iran 'will never bow to the enemy' while confirming all diplomatic paths remain open and that Iran is actively reviewing the latest US ceasefire proposal via the Pakistan channel.

outlets.name: IRNA
outlets.headline: Iran ISNA: US proposal has 'narrowed the gaps to some extent' — further progress requires US to end war temptation
outlets.summary: IRNA/ISNA reports Iran is formally reviewing the latest US ceasefire text via Pakistan mediator, with officials saying the proposal narrows differences but complete resolution requires Washington to abandon its military-threat posture.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Mojtaba Khamenei: 'Unprecedented historical epic of resistance against the two global terrorist armies'
outlets.summary: Iran International covers Mojtaba Khamenei's Day 83 statement marking the 2nd anniversary of Raisi's death, calling the US and Israel 'global terrorist armies' and framing the conflict as an 'unprecedented historical epic of resistance' — concurrent with Iran's more conciliatory diplomatic signals.
